Name: Dangerous
Notes:
(08/31/1987) (Bad is released)
(11/26/1991) (Dangerous is released)
Full Length: Dangerous was the first album released under the umbrella of Epic's new owners Sony Music, with a deal having been inked earlier that year. Though nine singles were released, the child molestation allegations of 1993 put a stop to the release of a planned tenth single. Over 70-75 songs were made for the album, which were eventually whittled down to 20 and later 14 tracks.

Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT. Supposedly inspired "Can't Let Her Get Away" and some of the other hard-hitting New Jack Swing tracks produced by Teddy Riley. Consists of car tyre screeches, low quality hits, and glass break snares. 

Snippet with voiceover leaked in a video made by Znop for the verb channel on October 23rd, 2025. Partially leaked on November 1st, 2025. Full song leaked in LQ on January 4th, 2026. Lossless file leaked later that day. You really can hear the garbage in this beat.

Name:
Homeless Bound
(prod. Brad Buxer & Bryan Loren)
(Homeless)
Notes:
Mainly consists of homeless people talking about their experiences over a half-industrial/half-jazzy musical bed. Sourced from an April 19th, 1990 DAT, features no vocals, and sonically sounds similar to "Heal The World." One user on MJJC who heard it at one of Brad's seminars described it as being "very experimental and rough." Allegedly revisited for the HIStory sessions. 

Heavily edited snippet leaked on November 3rd, 2025, with an unaltered CDQ snippet leaking later that day. A snippet that originally leaked on YouTube on August 31st, 2025 resurfaced on November 9th, 2025. Partially leaked in LQ as a seminar recording on January 9th, 2026 by John Cameron via his YouTube channel. More HQ snippets leaked shortly after.
Editor Note: If someone has a opus/webm rip of the August 31st snippet (or better quality than 192kbps), please reach out to us! Some of us are in a couple MJ Discord servers.

Name: Invincible
Notes:
(10/15/1997) (The HIStory World Tour ends, and Michael heads back to the studio in order to record his next album)
(10/30/2001) (Invincible is released)
Full Length: The result of a four-year labor of love that ended in a sense of disconnection and uneasiness amongst both Michael and his colleagues. Meant for a November 1999 release in order to bring Michael into the new millennium, it was first delayed to Fall 2000 and later October 2001, losing at least one track - "Shout" - in the process. Around 20-40 songs were produced for the album, with 16 making it into the final product. While still somewhat successful, this album was the lowest-selling in Michael's career.

Name: Post-Invincible
Notes:
(10/30/2001) (Invincible is released)
(06/13/2005) (Michael is declared "not guilty" on all 14 counts of child molestation)
Full Length: This particular era was marked with even more uncertainty concerning Michael's career and legacy. First he got into a dispute with Sony over how they handled the Invincible album and the ownership of his master tapes, resulting in him making plans to leave Sony. Then he was accused of sexually molesting Gavin Arvizo and taken to court, with the media taking the opportunity to mock him ruthlessly. In the end he was aquitted of all counts by the court, but Michael was left a broken man.

Name: Final sessions
Notes:
(06/13/2005) (Michael is declared "not guilty" on all 14 counts of child molestation)
(06/25/2009) (Michael Jackson dies of cardiac arrest)
Full Length: Once he was acquitted, Michael tried his best to get back into the music field. In 2005 he joined Two Seas Records and recorded some songs, but he left before anything substantial could be finished. The next year, he went to Ireland to work with famed producer Will. I. Am, expanding his horizons to producers such as Neff-U and RedOne in the years after. In March 2009, he announced a concert residency called "This Is It," but during rehearsals, he suddenly passed away on June 25th, 2009, from cardiac arrest.

Name: XSCAPE
Notes:
(12/10/2010) (The posthumous "Michael" album is released)
(05/09/2014) (XSCAPE is released)
Full Length: Released in 2014, XSCAPE started off strong, with its debut single "Love Never Felt So Good" proving very successful on the charts. Unfortunately, the botched launch of "A Place With No Name" as a single resulted in the album taking a hit, causing a deluxe edition featuring more songs to be canned at the last minute and the Estate to go into a hiatus of sorts. Around 24 songs were considered, with only 8 making it in.

Editor Comments

Era: Remember to use the right formatting. If you feel like something is missing or you did something wrong, copy something a previous submission has already done and use that as the guideline or just ask someone.
Notes:
Currently, the best sites for uploading files are:
Pillowcase, Pixeldrain & ImgBB.
No. Of Tracks: The universal tracker timezone is "EST". If something leaks, make sure to look at the EST timezone to get the date right.

Era: No fan remakes, no unofficial mixtapes, nothing that's commercially available, always upload to a site that can be streamed off of.
Notes: cobalt.tools is really good for downloading any type of video (YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo etc). Make sure to go to the settings and change "audio bitrate" to be 320kbps.
No. Of Tracks: All dates are to be written in "Month Day, Year" format as opposed to the "Month/Day/Year or Day/Month/Year" format.

Era: Make sure you use alternating colors and check often that they are not broken (Format, Alternating Colors, choose white and the grey that has one grey in between it and white.)
Notes: When linking to sources like web interviews, highlight over what is relevant with your mouse and then right click "Copy link to highlight". Doing so jumps straight to the part you highlighted and makes it stand out, which helps a ton.
No. Of Tracks: If you change the color/name of an era even slightly, make sure to apply the changes in every other page of the Tracker (Released, Stems, Album Copies & Misc). Same with adding/removing eras.

Era: No released remixes, no matter how obscure, should be on this page. Those should be in Released.
Notes: For sources with YouTube videos, go to the timestamp you need and right click "Copy Video URL at current time" instead of just copying the video. Use if it's stuff that needs it (e.g. long interviews).
No. Of Tracks: When adding sample info to an entry, make sure it's the earliest version with the sample that it's mentioned on or for certain entries it should be the Best Of / Special version.

Era: All music video editions, live freestyles, and released remixes go in Misc / Released.
Notes: Please note the difference between "High Quality" and "CD Quality", MP3 is NOT CD Quality.
No. Of Tracks: "Avaliable Length" matters more than "Quality", if there is a LQ partial snippet and a lossless snippet, the LQ partial takes presidence as it's longer.

Notes: "Rumored | Not Avaliable" always have N/A if they don't have a link, "Conficting Sources | Not Avaliable" and Confirmed | Not Avaliable" do not.
Do not write the name of the song in the notes column. Note down sample names and other songs if they need to be crossreferenced, but not the actual song name of the song in notes.
Please don't use the restore function if there's new info unless its extremely necessary.
